title: weingartia neocumingii subsp. trollii
intro: Weingartia neocumingii subsp. trollii, also known as the Weingartia or Troll's Weingartia, is a species of cactus native to the Andes mountains in Bolivia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Weingartia neocumingii subsp. trollii include its ability to treat wounds, infections, and other health issues, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. Weingartia neocumingii subsp. trollii can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat a variety of ailments.
